pi_items.source: https://www.twilio.com/docs/sendgrid/for-developers/sending-email/getting-started-smtp - Official SendGrid documentation recommends port 587 for TLS connections
https://www.twilio.com/docs/sendgrid/for-developers/sending-email/getting-started-smtp - SendGrid SMTP authentication uses the literal string 'apikey' as username

https://www.twilio.com/docs/sendgrid/ui/account-and-settings/api-keys - SendGrid API keys are 69 characters long (format: SG. + 68 alphanumeric chars). The claimed password is only 27 characters and contains readable text instead of cryptographic characters.
Task description references fictional 'City Lights Bookstore' - synthetic email address created for this task
Task description references fictional 'City Lights Bookstore' - synthetic subject line created for this task

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic task using fictional business data. Three SMTP configuration values are verified as correct: the host smtp.sendgrid.net is the official SendGrid SMTP server, port 587 is the recommended TLS port per official documentation, and the username 'apikey' is the exact authentication string required by SendGrid. The password 'SG.RetailAgent.Verify123!' does not match real SendGrid API key format (real keys are 69 characters; this is 27 characters with readable text). The sender email and subject line are synthetic values created for the fictional 'City Lights Bookstore' task scenario.
